_source: Transcribed from official ACA Skills Assessment documents (rev. 2/25/2025): Level 1 Introduction to Kayaking, Level 2 Essentials of Kayak Touring. Standards are the ACA criteria wording. 'optional: true' marks Developing/venue-specific skills that may be assessed but shall not count against the paddler.

levels.name: Level 1: Introduction to Kayaking
levels.note: Spray skirts are not appropriate for Level 1 kayaking courses.

skills.name: Secure kayak for transport
skills.standard: Make sure the kayak is securely fastened to the car or racks, using proper tie downs, straps, or knots.

skills.name: Lift & carry
skills.standard: Use proper techniques to safely lift and carry the kayak on shore.

skills.name: Weather & hazards
skills.standard: Obtain (recite) weather conditions, forecasts, and evaluate hazards to navigation and other environmental factors.

skills.name: Life jacket
skills.standard: Put on a life jacket appropriate for the operator's size, kayak, and activity; confirm all others in the group are properly wearing their life jacket.

skills.name: Inspect kayak & safety equipment
skills.standard: Inspect the kayak and all safety equipment to meet state, federal, and local requirements for the vessel and activity.

skills.name: Safe boating practices
skills.standard: Recognize, describe and follow safe boating practices (behavior, substance abuse, on-water and land etiquette, Leave No Trace ethics).

skills.name: Prepare for departure
skills.standard: Prepare kayak for departure, stowing gear securely, ensuring it is balanced.

skills.name: Float plan knowledge
skills.standard: Demonstrate knowledge of a float plan (components and filing).

skills.name: Paddle orientation & grip
skills.standard: Hold the paddle in correct orientation and grip for effective paddling.

skills.name: Principles of rescue
skills.standard: Demonstrate Principles of Rescue: safe rescue strategies (T-RETHROG: Talk, Reach, Throw, Row, Go); rescue priorities (people, kayak, paddle, gear); responsibilities of rescuer, group, and swimmers.

skills.name: Rescue & safety gear
skills.standard: Identify and demonstrate the use of appropriate rescue and safety gear for the kayak and planned activity.

skills.name: Wading risks
skills.standard: Understand the risks associated with standing and walking in these environments (including entanglement and environmental hazards).

skills.name: Swimming positions
skills.standard: Understand the pros and cons of different swimming positions.

skills.name: Swim with equipment
skills.standard: Self-rescue: swimming with equipment.

skills.name: Conserve energy
skills.standard: Strategies to conserve energy.

categories.name: Maneuvers & Strokes
categories.competency: Maneuvers to be performed with appropriate and efficient strokes, while maintaining the trim and balance of the craft.

skills.name: Launch from shore
skills.standard: Enter and launch from shore in a controlled manner, checking for clear departure.

skills.name: Forward, straight line
skills.standard: Propel the kayak forward in a straight line 15-20 kayak lengths.

skills.name: Stop
skills.standard: Stop the kayak within two kayak lengths.

skills.name: Reverse
skills.standard: Move the kayak backwards 3-4 kayak lengths.

skills.name: Draw sideways
skills.standard: Move the kayak sideways 10 feet (3 meters) to each side.

skills.name: Turn from stationary
skills.standard: Turn the kayak from a stationary position 180° to the right and left.

skills.name: Turn on the move
skills.standard: Turn the kayak while maintaining forward motion 90° to the right and left.

skills.name: Figure-8 course
skills.standard: Propel the kayak in a figure-of-8 course around markers 3-4 kayak lengths apart.

skills.name: Tripping
skills.standard: Experience paddling a day trip of at least short duration (1 hour) and distance (1 mile / 1.6 km).

skills.name: Land & exit
skills.standard: Arrive at destination point, checking for clear approach, and exit the kayak in a controlled and safe manner.

skills.name: Secure when unattended
skills.standard: Secure the kayak and equipment before leaving unattended, with attention to environment and conditions.

skills.name: Kayak types in rescues
skills.standard: Advantages and disadvantages during rescues of various kayaks (sit-ins, sit-on-tops, recreational kayaks).

skills.name: Wet exit
skills.standard: Calmly exit the kayak after capsize, using proper body position and contact with the kayak and paddle.

skills.name: Paddle recovery
skills.standard: Paddle recovery options.

skills.name: Kayak recovery
skills.standard: Strategies for kayak recovery.

skills.name: Swimmer tow options
skills.standard: Swimmer tow options.

skills.name: Re-entry / re-mount
skills.standard: Swimmer re-entry / re-mount techniques (stern entry, heel hook, rescue sling, paddle float).

skills.name: Bumping / bulldozing
skills.standard: Kayak bumping / bulldozing.

skills.name: Kayak towing
skills.standard: Kayak towing.

skills.name: Kayak-specific rescues
skills.standard: Kayak-specific rescues (boat over boat, boat to boat, scoop).

skills.name: Unresponsive paddler rescue
skills.standard: Unresponsive paddler rescue.

skills.name: Emptying water
skills.standard: Emptying water from kayak (on-shore, bailers, pumps).

skills.name: Equipment knowledge
skills.standard: Equipment: working knowledge of paddling equipment and terminology.

skills.name: Safety & hazard planning
skills.standard: Safety: understand the dangers of kayaking and how to plan an appropriate course to avoid potential hazardous situations.

skills.name: Cold water / thermal
skills.standard: Cold water shock, hypothermia and hyperthermia; prevention and treatment.

skills.name: Communication signals
skills.standard: Recognize and appropriately use communication (paddle and whistle) signals.

skills.name: Navigational rules
skills.standard: Follow basic navigational rules for inland waterways.

skills.name: Group awareness
skills.standard: Display awareness of the group and employ effective on-water management techniques.

levels.name: Level 2: Essentials of Kayak Touring
levels.note: Strokes, maneuvers, edging, and support are performed on both sides but need not be equally effective to 'meet' the standard at Level 2. Developing skills (optional) may add to a pass but shall not count against the paddler.

categories.name: Core: Strokes
categories.competency: The paddler displays connectivity between the paddle and kayak through safe and effective body movement, and a clean catch, power, and recovery of the blade at appropriate positions with correct blade orientation.

skills.name: Forward Paddling
skills.standard: Moves the kayak 200 yards/meters in a reasonably straight line.

skills.name: Reverse Paddling
skills.standard: Moves the kayak 50 yards/meters with reasonable control.

skills.name: Stopping
skills.standard: Stops the kayak from a good speed (2 to 2.5 knots), in forward and reverse.

skills.name: Forward & Reverse Sweep
skills.standard: Turns the kayak smoothly while moving forward or in reverse without losing momentum.

categories.name: Core: Maneuvers
categories.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.

skills.name: Rotate in place 360°
skills.standard: Spins the kayak in place with minimal forward or reverse movement.

skills.name: Turning on the Move
skills.standard: Maneuvers the kayak smoothly around obstacles.

skills.name: Draw Sideways
skills.standard: Moves the kayak sideways 10 feet (3.3 meters).

skills.name: Ruddering (Stern)
skills.standard: Holds the kayak on a straight course or turns toward the paddle side to correct a course.

skills.name: Stern Rudder (away side)
skills.standard: Turns away from the paddle side while stern ruddering.

skills.name: Linked Maneuvers
skills.standard: Paddles a course requiring various turning maneuvers safely, effectively, and efficiently.

categories.name: Core: Edging and Support
categories.competency: The paddler applies consistent and purposeful edging to make turning strokes more effective and efficient. They can stabilize their kayak, with and without momentum, and prevent capsizing.

skills.name: Low Brace Recovery
skills.standard: Prevents capsize when off balance.

skills.name: Low Brace Turn
skills.standard: Makes a turn of at least 90 degrees.

skills.name: Edge Control
skills.standard: Edges with purpose and consistency during strokes and maneuvers.

skills.name: Turn Control
skills.standard: Able to vary the radius of their turns.

categories.name: Core: Awareness and Seamanship
categories.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group comfort.

skills.name: Awareness
skills.standard: Demonstrates awareness of self, group members, and environment.

skills.name: Leave No Trace
skills.standard: Interacts appropriately with the ecosystem and wildlife.

skills.name: Kayak
skills.standard: Working knowledge of types of kayaks suitable for paddling in Level 2 conditions.

skills.name: Equipment
skills.standard: Working knowledge of paddling equipment.

skills.name: Cold-Water Shock
skills.standard: Understands cold-water shock and the sudden onset of issues associated with immersion in cold water.

skills.name: Hypothermia & Hyperthermia
skills.standard: Working knowledge of the issues associated with both, including prevention, recognition, and basic treatment.

categories.name: Core: Incident Prevention and Management
categories.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring, and can manage an incident when it does happen by having appropriate equipment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact and how in the case of an incident requiring outside assistance.

skills.name: Securing Kayak to Rack
skills.standard: Properly secures their kayak to rack when storing or transporting.

skills.name: Lifting and Carrying
skills.standard: Lifts and carries their kayak safely and efficiently.

skills.name: Launching and Landing
skills.standard: Launches and lands at a dock or shore safely.

skills.name: Signaling
skills.standard: Carries and demonstrates proper use of an audible signaling device.

skills.name: Communication Device
skills.standard: Has a reliable communication device readily available and knows proper device usage.

skills.name: Float Plan
skills.standard: Files a complete and accurate float plan with an appropriate point of contact.

categories.name: Core: Trip Planning and Navigation
categories.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Their planning includes weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members' equipment.

skills.name: Forecasts
skills.standard: Gathers, understands, and utilizes appropriate weather and marine forecasts.

skills.name: Chart Use
skills.standard: Demonstrates use of charts, aerial images, weather and marine forecasts, and knowledge of their paddling group when route planning.

skills.name: Trip Planning — wind/waves/currents
skills.standard: Incorporates wind, waves, and currents into route plan and trip choices.

skills.name: Trip Planning — direction of flow
skills.standard: Incorporates direction of flow into route plan and trip choices.

skills.name: Route Finding
skills.standard: Uses appropriate map or chart to know where they are and where they are going.

skills.name: Compass Use
skills.standard: Demonstrates using handheld and deck-mounted compasses to take bearings.

skills.name: Nautical Rules of the Road
skills.standard: Understands the Nautical Rules of the Road and demonstrates safe paddling in relation to boat traffic.

skills.name: Tide & Tidal Drift
skills.standard: Demonstrates ability to calculate tide and tidal drift stages and incorporate results into a trip plan.

categories.name: Core: Rescues and Towing
categories.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.

skills.name: Capsize & Wet Exit
skills.standard: Performs a wet exit in two ways.

skills.name: Assisted Rescues & Deep-Water Re-Entry
skills.standard: Performs one or more assisted rescues as rescuer and swimmer: Side-by-Side, Sling, or T-Rescue.

skills.name: Self-Rescue
skills.standard: Performs one of: Scramble (cowboy), Paddle Float, or Re-enter and Roll (with or without paddle float).

skills.name: Swim Rescue
skills.standard: Swims to shore (25 yards/meters) in full paddling gear and drains kayak.

skills.name: Move a Capsized Kayak to Shore
skills.standard: Moves a capsized kayak ~3 kayak lengths (~50 ft/15 m) via Bulldozing, Shove and Chase, or Contact Tow.

skills.name: Swimmer Tows
skills.standard: Performs swimmer rescue via a bow, toggle, or back deck swimmer carry.

skills.name: Scoop Rescue
skills.standard: Able to assist a swimmer into their kayak.

skills.name: Unresponsive Paddler Rescue
skills.standard: Able to bring a capsized paddler to the surface.

skills.name: Towing
skills.standard: Able to use towlines and various towing configurations to move a kayak.

categories.name: Venue (Developing): Currents
categories.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 currents while maintaining control of their kayak.

skills.name: Currents — Awareness
skills.standard: Demonstrates understanding of currents (tidal or river) in Level 2 venues.

skills.name: Currents — Reading Water & Boat Control
skills.standard: Identifies direction of flow and eddies and paddles through currents in control.

skills.name: Currents — Hazards
skills.standard: Identifies hazards and positions themselves to avoid hazards.

skills.name: Currents — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing in current.

categories.name: Venue (Developing): Wind and Waves
categories.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 wind and waves while maintaining control of their kayak.

skills.name: Wind/Waves — Awareness
skills.standard: Understands how onshore and offshore winds affect paddling conditions.

skills.name: Wind/Waves — Tactics
skills.standard: Applies strategies for paddling at various angles to the direction of the wind.

skills.name: Wind/Waves — Boat Control
skills.standard: Can control their direction and maintain stability in wind and waves.

skills.name: Wind/Waves — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing in wind and waves.

categories.name: Venue (Developing): Rocky Shorelines
categories.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently along rocky shorelines in Level 2 conditions, identifying safe and danger zones, self-rescuing close to rock features, performing assisted rescues, and contact towing where appropriate.

skills.name: Rocky — Awareness
skills.standard: Identifies hazards and landing areas on a map or chart.

skills.name: Rocky — Tactics
skills.standard: Chooses appropriate landings and areas to play in the rocks.

skills.name: Rocky — Boat Control
skills.standard: Maneuvers along rocky shorelines safely, effectively, and efficiently.

skills.name: Rocky — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing close to rock features.
